page.title: Cookie Simulator
page.description: Adjust the ingredient levels, click Bake, and see what kind of cookie you created.

ingredients.label: Butter
ingredients.description: Controls spread. More butter usually makes cookies flatter.

ingredients.label: Flour
ingredients.description: Adds structure. More flour usually makes cookies thicker.

ingredients.label: Brown Sugar
ingredients.description: Adds moisture and makes cookies softer or chewier.

recipes.explanation: High butter increases spread and low flour gives the dough less structure, so the cookie bakes flat.
High brown sugar adds moisture and medium-to-high flour gives enough structure, so the cookie turns out chewy.
Low butter limits spread while high flour adds structure, so the cookie stays thick.
High butter and high white sugar encourage spread and crispiness, while medium flour keeps the cookie from getting too thick.

fallback_result.explanation: This combination did not create a recognizable cookie type. Try adjusting your ingredient levels and baking again.
